review_title: Powerful Tool for IT performance management
description: My overall experience with Galileo has been postive. It provides strong monitoring capabilities and centralized visibility which makes managing things easier . It does offer useful insights and helps quickly identify issue
author: Pankaj T.
pros: What i like most about Galileo is its ability to brings everything in one place . It brings all perfomance data into one centralized platform . It simplifies monitering by offering real time insights.
cons: The major downsides for me would be the the dashboard and customization options as they can feel overwhelming initially

review_title: Data Visibility to drive Cost Optimization
description: I have worked with Galileo since 2020. The team behind the product is extraordinarily smart on IT infrastructure and what it takes to monitor and measure performance, capacity, and utilization. Very smart, customer-focused engineering teams make deploying and supporting Galileo low risk and cost effective
author: Andrew F.
pros: It is built by a company that built the product to support its consulting business. It is focused on the collecting the essential data to provide critical data visibility to support rapid root cause analysis and resolution and provide capacity, performance and utilization data key to eliminate IT overspend and increasing asset utilization.
cons: There is nothing not to like about Galileo! Galileo is a robust, highly available product for enterprise Observability and Telemetry. Licensing is cost effective and easy to understand and scale across the enterprise

review_title: Galileo Monitoring is a lot to take on (but mostly in a good way)!
description: Galileo has been a lot more challenging for us to implement than we anticipated. Demonstrations showed off the value of the Galileo Suite, and we remain invested in it as a significant improvement over the collection of disparate monitoring tools we used to have, but getting it set up the way we want has been taking longer than expected. The implementation services provided by the vendor have been good, but it's required a delicate balance of how much we have them do for us, vs. how much we try to take on ourselves so that we get the knowledge transfer needed to move forward in the future on our own. After several months, we still feel like we're learning. In the end it's a great product at a good price and worth investing the time in, but don't expect it to achieve its full potential by just slapping it in place with default options. Plan some time to do it right, and you'll be rewarded.
author: Eric G.
pros: The vendor has deep experience and a strong reputation with SAN storage and IBM iSeries and AIX server workloads. Both the internally-developed and open-source-based tools that comprise the Galileo suite come with customizations that provide excellent support for these environments, which many other monitoring vendors struggle to do at all, much less do correctly. Meanwhile, support for more common platforms like Windows and Linux remains strong, meaning Galileo is an excellent platform for consolidating many other disparate monitoring platforms without sacrificing the capabilities found in more "native" monitoring solutions.
cons: There's a fairly steep learning curve, especially with the Active Monitoring product based on Zabbix. Most monitoring products have a well-known approach, setting up individual hosts and associated services, and defining simple alerting thresholds for each service. With Galileo, there's considerably more flexibility to define trigger actions based on complex rules. On the plus side this allows for useful customizations other tools don't, but it also means even the most basic implementations need to adapt to this more complex methodology, writing rules rather than just setting simple thresholds, and learning to account for event handling for every threshold transition, both up and down. Host templates provided out of the box are expansive, and should be approached carefully to avoid being overwhelmed by alerting early in implementation; a "build up what you need" rather than "pare down from the defaults" approach may be helpful to contain alerting sprawl.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great performance monitoring infrastructure platform for Linux servers
description: This three 3 years that I've been working at Carhartt have been a great pleasure using Galileo as a daily working tool because we can trust and rely on the complete performance monitoring of the Linux servers.
author: Mario H.
pros: This is a great monitoring platform what allow us to have observability of our servers' health and asset management. I want to share this platform has ease of use and ease of install/upgrade agents for Linux servers. And give us the power of resolve health issues based on alerts that comes to us.
cons: Sometimes the assets seen duplicated due to an internal processes but nothing that cannot be fixed by Galileo's support team.

review_title: Innovative and challenging : My time at Galileo
description: Working on Galileo within IQOR has been a mixed experience , with both challenges and opportunities . On the positive side, the company provided a collaborative environment where i was able to learn a lot from my colleagues. The team culture is generally supportive , and i appreciated the chance to work on diverse projects that helped me build a variety of skills.
author: Arnold D.
pros: What i like about Galileo is how easy it is to use. In a busy environment like ours at IQor , it's important to have something that doesn't look like forever to learn . the interface is clean and simple , so i can jump right into my task without getting bogged down by complicated menus or unnecessary features.
cons: Depending on your role , the workload at Galileo could became overwhelming . while the company strive to support employees, there were times when balancing personal and professional life felt challenging, especially during peak times. The internal tools and system sometimes weren't as efficient as expected , leading to minor frustration when trying to complete the task quickly .

description: I love the easy to read graphs that allow a quick visual on the health of a system. This has helped with identifying root cause of issue.
author: Gary B.
pros: As a semi-technical user it is great that I can quickly identify potential issues and share with the technical team to get resolutions. The ability to go back in time to find hints of when an issue may have started before it reached a critical level is a big help in isolating cause of issue.
cons: No drill down ability. If I could see what pid was building a large data set or taking a lot of memory. I would love to automated alerts for potential issues.

description: My experience with the Galileo system has been positive in terms of its stability, availability, and wide range of functionality for IT infrastructure use. However, issues like data inconsistency between API and UI and occasional lack of real-time synchronization can impact reliability in practical scenarios.
author: Aman V.
pros: - The Galileo platform is designed to provide consistent availability and stable performance, making it reliable for critical IT infrastructure operations. - It offers multiple features such as precise timing, positioning data, and integration capabilities, which support various IT infrastructure needs like synchronization, monitoring, and system coordination.
cons: - In the Galileo system, discrepancies may occur between data fetched via APIs and what is displayed on the UI, leading to confusion and potential errors in decision-making. - Differences in update timing between backend (API) and frontend (UI) can result in outdated or mismatched information being shown to users.

author: Anthony A.
pros: The UI is simple to use. I am able to nagivate around fairly easy and always know where everything is located
cons: I wish there was more options for drill downs. I want to be able to make some custom reports without having to ask for help from support.

review_title: A Powerful Infrastructure Monitoring Platform with Deep Analytics
description: Overall, it was good with Galileo Suite. As it is powerful solution for organization that really need deep monitoring into their IT systems and detect some performance issues, optimizing infrastructure usage.
author: Meshv P.
pros: Galileo gives excellent visibility for complex infrastructure and helps to monitor servers, applications and cloud resources in one place. I really like about real time monitoring and some analytics features, which help to find performance issues and plan future infrastructure capacity.
cons: The platform can take some time to learn as new users, mostly for teams that are totally new into enterprise infrastructure tools.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: I have enjoyed using the product.
author: Nicole M.
pros: How it allows me to predict when a client will need to expand their CPU, Disk or Memory.
cons: Nothing.

description: its is a great product with amazing sales, support, and development teams' backing it
author: sandy W.
pros: its ease of use, and details of performance and you can see your infrastructure in one location
cons: wish we were able to alert on Storage. ie.. when one or a few metrics spiked - i.e Host Cluster Service time spiked over 2milliseconds.. But we have started working with support and development to get this implemented
author_profession: Sr. Manager, IBM i and Storage

description: Really well, support rocks
author: Michael K.
pros: Overall Views into our Datacenter, supports many OS
cons: Always having to resync Azure Vms and send in emails to correct duplicate issues

description: It’s a powerful tool, but it takes time to get used to. Perfect for teams who need detailed insights but may require some patience to navigate.
author: Heldor G.
pros: I really liked how Galileo gives a clear view of everything happening in your systems.
cons: The many features they offer might feel overwhelming.

description: It's great. Galileo intuitive and easy to implement! Those qualities are definitely standout features that make it appealing to users, especially when it comes to complex systems like natural gas infrastructure. The user-friendly interface and ease of navigation are crucial for engineers and decision-makers who need to quickly access data and make informed decisions without spending too much time on a steep learning curve. Being able to implement the software smoothly without disrupting existing workflows is also a major advantage, especially in industries where time and precision are critical.
author: Laheba S.
pros: for quick issue detection and preventing outages—those are some of the most critical aspects of managing energy systems like natural gas networks. The ability to identify potential issues early and take preventive measures can save a lot of time, reduce costs, and most importantly, improve safety. The fact that accuracy in measurement is a standout feature for you is really important, especially in industries like natural gas management, where even small discrepancies can lead to significant operational challenges. When you’re working with such precise data, you can trust your decisions and avoid costly mistakes that might arise from inaccurate readings.
cons: When software is both intuitive and perfectly suited to your needs, it can make a huge difference in productivity and overall efficiency. It’s great that it’s meeting all your expectations. I love Galileo

description: It helps you see your devices and their health easily. The 3D views make finding problems quick. It helps fix issues fast. It’s useful, but can take time to learn.
author: Shivam S.
pros: It shows all your device health in one place. The 3D views make finding problems easy. It helps fix issues quickly and keeps things working well.
cons: The setup at the beginning may be hard for some.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: Overall experience has been positive as it provides powerful tools for IT capacity and performance management. Its data visualization and predictive analytics capabilities have been instrumental in identifying usage trends and planning future infrastructure needs.
author: Pranjal M.
pros: The predictive analytics it provides in order to predict IT usage trends, forecast future trends and prevent capacity bottlenecks. Also, provides access to extensive historical data, enabling users to identify long-term trends and make data-backed decisions.
cons: There are occasional data sync delays. Also learning curve is steep and require significant time and training to master. Pricing is also one of the concerns.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Easy to install and upgrade. Easy to set up worksheets in GUI
author: Teresa P.
pros: Ease of use. Able to see all systems within 1 location on GUI
cons: I see no Cons for this product. It is a great product and Bob is a wonderful trainer

description: I like the product and have been using it for several years, back to about 2016. It has a ton of real time data and can help you manage your enterprise better. It just takes time to learn how to use.
author: Andrew H.
pros: Real time data helps us troubleshoot with current data. Its ability to work with all of our storage device types makes integration easy. Its reporting trends helps us be proactive with our storage capacity planning.
cons: It sometimes takes a while to figure out how to find what you are looking for. This is good and bad. Its all there, its just a matter of finding out where to look for what you need. It takes time to dig around and figure out how it works.

review_title: A great tool with capabilities far beyond our ability to understand and exploit them
description: Overall, Galileo has been very useful to us, relatively easy to use at the basic level at which we use it, and the vendor support has been outstanding.
author: Michael S.
pros: Great vendor support - very knowledgeable and extremely willing to explain and assist.Great graphing and charting; flexible enough to do almost anything if you take the time to learn how. It did not take us long to activate the features which we needed most, and it has been very reliable and robust, requiring very little maintenance.
cons: It is frustrating that for real-time monitoring, very few metrics are available for which we can view current data which would help with problem diagnosis like a performance problem. (many exist which are of little use to us) . Compared to non-realtime data, almost nothing important is immediately available. We should be able to choose which metrics to collect in order to trade off usefulness (to us) against collection time.It is perceived that to make use of any more functionality would require a large investment of time. We are probably doing it wrong, but we find it very hard to add new systems and configure them into our existing notifications - we have to do them one by one and feature by feature rather than being able to add a list of hosts to an existing object or capability.All systems send data to the cloud at the same time and that can result in some performance issues.We would like to be able to send alerts to distribution lists defined and controlled at our site so that the Galileo configuration does not have to change (e.g. the on-call paging), but with the monitoring hosted at AWS, email alerts take so many hops in AWS to our site and back out to MS365 ( hosted elsewhere) and through those mail hosts, that email alerts are rejected by cloud mail servers (over which we have no control) because they have been forwarded too many times ; we cannot leverage our on-site DLs and have to manually update the addresses in the Galileo GUI, which is tedious

review_title: Early Adopter of Galileo, Troy University
author: William P.
pros: As early adopters of the Galileo Suite, Troy University has significantly benefited from its robust IT infrastructure monitoring capabilities. The tool's predictive analytics and custom dashboards have been instrumental in proactively identifying system performance issues, preventing potential downtime. Its secure, agentless architecture and insightful exploratory analytics have streamlined our IT operations, enhancing efficiency and reliability across our campus networks.
cons: Exceptionally pleased with the tool, no complaints.

description: Galileo has found bottle necks in our network environment and help resolve issues with our customers by the numerus and excellent charts provided by Galileo.
author: Nathan K.
pros: I like that I can see from end to end via storage arrays to core switch's, to the hosts. I also like that Galileo can see into multiple vendors and provide the detailed data with very fast response time.
cons: Not FED ramped. We were not able to leverage galileo in the federal cloud as a service.

review_title: A product that works as expected for Monitoring
description: Exceptional we use daily exec reporting to identify potential issues
author: Earl W.
pros: It monitors a variety of OSes and is comprehensive with details and summary
cons: I have no dislikes at all - products just work

description: My experience with Galileo has been fantastic. I don't recall ever having an issue with usage and availability.
author: Brian T.
pros: The few times I needed assistance from support it was a good experience allowing private one on one time with support to work thru the issue to resolution.Having the history of the data available has been extremely beneficial.
cons: There are other features that I haven't tapped into yet such as reporting aspects or customized reports which I am unsure how to get started and what is offered.A heatmap is a feature that I would like to see implemented.

description: I was the initial PM on the Galileo POC. Working with [sensitive content hidden] and others at Galileo has been great from the start. I've been in the IT infrastructure filed for 35+ years and they aren't a typical technology company. They are just as engaged post sale as pre-sale. Once purchased and deployed we had numerous Galileo led technical sessions on how to get the most out of the product. When we need a report Galileo showed us where we could gather the info or they created and customized a report just for us. We find their pricing very reasonable and we've not seen any large increases in license fees since we purchased. We're currently looking into a Galileo site license to expand our trouble shooting, capacity planning, reporting and governance capablilties.
author: Derek M.
pros: Galileo provides our company numerous benefits. We have the Galileo AIX agents installed on all of our AIX machines. We use Galileo to do real-time troubleshooting, performance management and reporting. The tool is also used by our governance teams to ensure our service provider is meeting our SLA and KPIs. Our service provider uses Galileo to gather data they can't get from the native in house tools. Reporting on the environment is critical in my role and the Galileo staff has been more than willing to create and customize reports for myself and numerous other areas.
cons: I have no real cons related to the Galileo product.

description: I am able to review performance information and use it to troubleshoot issues. i like that the data remains in Galileo for as long as it's collected and you can revert back to old data for comparison.
author: Mark D.
pros: I like that Galileo supports multiple platforms, including cloud. I use it for problem determination, scoping growth, troubleshooting, report generation. I have it installed on my IBM iSeries partitions as well as my AIX/VIO/Linux machines as well.
cons: If anything, I think there should be a centralized page describing what each feature of Galileo does- Explorer, Cloud Compass, Observatory, etc. For users that are new to the product, understanding what each of these can show you is important.
